caption:
Your CRM runs one response sequence. Your leads don't have one level of urgency.

Sort every lead into one of three buckets before you decide how fast to respond.

HOT: called, texted after hours, or asked for a price today. Respond in minutes or lose them to the next name on the list.

WARM: filled a form or asked a question with no deadline. They need reminders and proof over days, not a same-minute call.

COLD: past customers, list subscribers, casual followers. They need a reason to come back, not speed.

Industry research on response time shows answering a hot lead within 5 minutes can roughly double your close rate. Only 17% of service businesses answer a new lead within an hour. That stat means nothing for a cold lead getting a quarterly email.

Tag by temperature first. Build the response speed second.

DM "SORT"
#LeadFollowUp #ServiceBusinessGrowth #SpeedToLead #CRMAutomation #HomeServiceMarketing
rationale: Carousels currently lead Instagram saves and depth for educational/authority content, and this fills Authority/Frameworks with an unclaimed triage model (lead temperature, not the four-system self-score or forensic diagnostic already shipped).
